pages.title: About Louise - Louise Haigh MP - Louise Haigh MP
pages.text_excerpt: About Louise - Louise Haigh MP - Louise Haigh MP Skip to main content Louise Haigh MP Fighting for Sheffield's future Home About Louise News Policy Campaigns In Parliament Contact Me Local Business Champions 2025 Cost-of-Living – help and support Mental Health Services Gleadless Valley Masterplan More Search Search the website Search Menu About Louise Louise Haigh MP To read Louise’s privacy policy, click here. For details of Parliamentary expenses, click here Louise was first elected as the Member of Parliament for Sheffield Heeley in May 2015 . She was born and brought up on Abbeydale Road and now lives in Norfolk Park. Louise made clear in her acceptance speech the people of Sheffield Heeley will always be her number one priority. After her first year as an MP, she was named by independent House of Commons Library research as the hardest-working of all new MPs. In the years since, she’s stood up for local people and held the Government to account on everything from special educational needs and reform of the family courts to fracking and children’s mental health . In July 2017, Louise was appointed Shadow Policing Minister and, drawing on her experience as a former Special Constable, campaigned for the Police to be properly resourced, staffed, and accountable to the public . Louise was appointed as Shadow Secretary of State for Northern Ireland within Labour’s Shadow Cabinet in April 2020. Labour has a proud record in Northern Ireland and Louise has renewed the party’s historic commitment to work as honest brokers and trusted partners with political leaders, businesses and communities. Louise was made Shadow Secretary of State for Transport in November 2021, where she has prioritised ensuring public transport is affordable, accessible and reliable. At last year’s Labour Party Conference Louise announced Labour’s commitment to bring our railways into public ownership and to allow local areas to establish their own municipal bus companies. In July 2024 Louise was appointed Secretary of State for Transport. Furthermore in this role Louise has been a vocal advocate for the rights of workers. Most recently by firmly holding the Government to account for their handling of the rail workers’ industrial dispute and leading the fight to protect the rights of P&O workers following their illegal firing. Before becoming an MP, Louise served as a shop steward for the Unite union to help bring about change in the workplace. As a proud trade unionist, she’s supported workers facing discrimination, harassment, redundancy and unfair dismissal. Louise will continue to champion the issues which matter to her constituents. If you need any advice and support, Louise holds regular advice surgeries in the constituency where you can raise any issues. Meet the team I have staff in my constituency office and in Westminster. Across the two offices, I have seven full-time members of staff who help me to effectively represent my constituents both locally and in Parliament. It’s important to me that I’m able to take up the issues that matter most to local residents so that I can properly fulfil my responsibilities as your MP and my staff are on hand to offer help, advice and assistance to constituents. You can read more about them below: Anita is my Office Manager Anita is responsible for the day-to-day running of the office and my diary. She can answer any of your queries and help arrange meetings between local groups and myself. Along with helping me with press enquiries and media, Anita manages surveys and consultations so I can hear from my constituents about what matters most to them. Anita also helps to arrange visits from local schools so please get in touch if your school is in the constituency and you would like to visit Parliament. Laura is my Senior Caseworker If you have an issue or problem that needs quick action and urgent attention, Laura is here to help. Laura attends surgeries with me and helps me gets to the bottom of issues that arise

headings: h2: Community Safety in your area
h2: Community safety in your area
h2: Clamping Down on Unfair Parking Tickets
h2: Fighting for Better Social Care
h2: More Funding for SEND Children
h2: Free Travel for Young Carers
pages.text_excerpt: Campaigns - Louise Haigh MP - Louise Haigh MP Skip to main content Louise Haigh MP Fighting for Sheffield's future Home About Louise News Policy Campaigns In Parliament Contact Me Local Business Champions 2025 Cost-of-Living – help and support Mental Health Services Gleadless Valley Masterplan More Search Search the website Search Menu Campaigns On this page you can find details of my recent campaign work. Community Safety in your area I want to find out more about the issues that are affecting you in your community and what you like most about the area in which you live. I will use the information you provide to me to work with the relevant agencies to help make improvements to the area and to celebrate all that is good about the constituency. Have you say today Big Green Conversation The UK will host the 26 th UN Climate Change Conference of the Parties (COP26) from the 31 October through to the 12 November. The climate talks will bring together heads of state, climate experts and campaigners to agree coordinated action to tackle climate change. These will be the most important talks about climate action for a generation. It’s vital we all reach an ambitious agreement. I want to hear from constituents about your views and priorities about how to address climate change. I also want you to share with me what small changes you are making to help address the climate crisis, because small changes can make a big difference. Share your pledges with me here. Stop the Cut Shock new figures show a staggering 513,414 people will be hit by the cut to Universal Credit in Yorkshire, including almost 50,000 in Sheffield alone. Double the figure from March 2020, as the economic damage of the pandemic is revealed. For many families, the extra £20 a week will be the difference between whether they can put food on the table and heat their homes this winter. It is shameful that some of the very people who were on the frontline during this pandemic, low-paid nurses, police officers, teaching assistants, are now set to be plunged into hardship. Please show your support against the Governments unjust decision to cut Universal Credit by signing my petition . If the reduction does go ahead, as the Government plans, it will cost six million families an estimated £1,000 a year and support for those out of work will be left at its lowest level in decades. I cannot and will not stand back and allow it to go ahead without a fight. Better Buses App More than 100,000 people across Sheffield don’t own a car and many more rely on our bus network to get them to work, leisure and appointments. I have launched an app for residents and passengers so you can log issues with the bus network. The information gathered will be used to challenge the bus operators and to work with Mayor of the Sheffield City Region to demand improvements. Via the Better Buses app passengers can provide their feedback about specific services, how often they’re delayed or cancelled, the condition of bus stops and issues they experience with their journey such as hate crime or abuse. To report issues with your bus services visit: betterbuses.LouiseHaigh.org.uk/ and download the app onto your phone so you can report issues in real-time and on the go. Community safety in your area Over the past few months many of you have been contacting me about anti-social behaviour in your neighbourhoods and communities, including the use of illegal off-road bikes, groups causing intimidation and noise pollution. It’s a real frustration to see these problems getting worse again. After several years hard work, we had started to get on top if it, but clearly insufficient youth provision and our policing resources still being stretched, this is having an effect. That’s why, this summer I will be out in the community speaking to you about your views of safety in your community, what you think the priorities should be and how they should be tackled. Have your say today and help me to make our communities safe

campaigns.title: Community Safety in your area
campaigns.summary: I want to find out more about the issues that are affecting you in your community and what you like most about the area in which you live. I will use the information you provide to me to work with the relevant agencies to help make improvements to the area and to celebrate all that is good about the constituency.

campaigns.title: Big Green Conversation
campaigns.summary: The UK will host the 26 th UN Climate Change Conference of the Parties (COP26) from the 31 October through to the 12 November. The climate talks will bring together heads of state, climate experts and campaigners to agree coordinated action to tackle climate change. These will be the most important talks about climate action for a generation. It’s vital we all reach an ambitious agreement.

campaigns.title: Stop the Cut
campaigns.summary: Shock new figures show a staggering 513,414 people will be hit by the cut to Universal Credit in Yorkshire, including almost 50,000 in Sheffield alone. Double the figure from March 2020, as the economic damage of the pandemic is revealed.

campaigns.title: Better Buses App
campaigns.summary: More than 100,000 people across Sheffield don’t own a car and many more rely on our bus network to get them to work, leisure and appointments.

campaigns.title: Community safety in your area
campaigns.summary: Over the past few months many of you have been contacting me about anti-social behaviour in your neighbourhoods and communities, including the use of illegal off-road bikes, groups causing intimidation and noise pollution.

campaigns.title: Clamping Down on Unfair Parking Tickets
campaigns.summary: I’ve launched a campaign against rip-off private car park vendors after hearing from several local people who have wrongly received steep parking fines.

campaigns.title: We’re Still Viable
campaigns.summary: I’ve launched a campaign to support local businesses that have been badly hit by the pandemic, lockdown, and local restrictions.

campaigns.title: Fighting for Better Social Care
campaigns.summary: I was a founding member of the new All-Party Parliamentary Group on Social Care and used my position on the APPG to push for much-needed change of the social care system .

campaigns.title: Defending Survivors of Abuse
campaigns.summary: It’s been a long road since I met with the amazing Rotherham survivor, Sammy Woodhouse, and we started campaigning for reform to the family courts , which have been allowed to perpetuate abuse against victims for too long.

campaigns.title: More Funding for SEND Children
campaigns.summary: I’ve been speaking to parents, schools, and local authorities about the lack of provision for children with special educational needs and disabilities (SEND). I’ve been working locally and in Parliament to make sure that children with SEND and their families get all the support they need. Find out more.

campaigns.title: Free Travel for Young Carers
campaigns.summary: I have been campaigning for Sheffield’s 7,000 young carers to receive free bus travel, in order to allow them to access school, work, socialisation and their caring responsibilities without extra financial pressure. They give a lot to look after their loved ones, and it’s only right they receive something in return. Find out more .

pages.title: Contact Me - Louise Haigh MP - Louise Haigh MP
pages.text_excerpt: Contact Me - Louise Haigh MP - Louise Haigh MP Skip to main content Louise Haigh MP Fighting for Sheffield's future Home About Louise News Policy Campaigns In Parliament Contact Me Local Business Champions 2025 Cost-of-Living – help and support Mental Health Services Gleadless Valley Masterplan More Search Search the website Search Menu Contact Me Thank you so much for getting in touch with me. I receive hundreds of emails, letters and phone calls each day and my team do our very best to respond as quickly as possible, but the volume is growing. I aim to respond to all correspondence from constituents as swiftly as possible. Based on our current casework volumes, we aim to respond to you within ten working days and emergency cases will be prioritised. Constituents first: Due to the volume of emails I receive and the need to prioritise urgent casework, we don’t have capacity to respond to emails that aren’t relevant with my role as Member of Parliament for Sheffield Heeley. Checklist: Please check you are one of my constituents: There is a strict parliamentary protocol that MPs do not pursue issues raised by or about constituents of other MPs. So, before my team can process your enquiry, please check that you are one of my constituents. You can do this by entering your postcode here. You should also ensure that you have: Provided your full name. Provided your full postal address including post code. Provided a contact telephone number and an email address if you have one. Provided your national insurance number (where relevant) Attached copies of any supporting documents or media (where relevant). IMPORTANT: If you have not included this information, please re-send your email as I am unable to process your enquiry without it. Contact Details You can get in touch with me by post, phone, email, or by using the form below. If your issue is urgent then please call us on the phone. Address: House of Commons, London, SW1A 0AA Office hours: The office is open Monday to Friday from 9am to 5pm, but please ring ahead before visiting. Constituency Phone number: 0114 250 8113 Contact Email address: louise.haigh.mp@parliament.uk Twitter: twitter.com/LouHaigh Facebook: facebook.com/LouiseHaighMP LinkedIN: www.linkedin.com/in/louise-haigh-mp-8a5362282 How I can help I can help constituents with the following issues: Tax problems involving the HM Revenue and Customs Department Welfare issues dealt with by the Department for Work and Pensions such as benefits, tax credits and state pensions. Issues handled by the Home Office such as immigration and passports. Issues relating to the Department of Health & Social Care such as concerns about hospitals or access to treatment. Any issues arising from any other government departments. Issues with utility companies. Consumer issues. You have a policy issue that you want me to raise with the Government I can support constituents by: Writing to the Minister with responsibility for the department concerned. Asking an official for an explanation of why a decision has been made or policy position taken. Asking for a decision to be reviewed. Help with council issues IMPORTANT: Issues relating to services provided by Sheffield Council need to be raised with the relevant council department. This includes: Housing and council tax Anti-social behaviour Highways and roads Planning Bins and littering School placements Adults’ and children’s social services You can find out more about council services here. Invitations: I receive many invitations and we try to offer apologies where I am unable to attend, and I prioritise invitations that are relevant to my constituents. As an MP my diary reacts to urgent issues and on occasion I may need to postpone or cancel meetings or attending an event, sometimes at short notice. Where possible my team will try and rearrange. Please accept my apologies if you don’t receive a response to an event invite within two weeks. Policy If you have contacted me about a policy related is

pages.text_excerpt: Cost-of-Living - help and support - Louise Haigh MP - Louise Haigh MP Skip to main content Louise Haigh MP Fighting for Sheffield's future Home About Louise News Policy Campaigns In Parliament Contact Me Local Business Champions 2025 Cost-of-Living – help and support Mental Health Services Gleadless Valley Masterplan More Search Search the website Search Menu Cost-of-Living – help and support Cost-of-living resource The cost-of-living crisis is a national emergency that will affect us all, as across Sheffield and the country households face energy bills they can’t afford. I’ve created an online resource which collates the details of organisations, services and charities that are available to help people manage their finances. Also included are a list of mental health charities based across the city to help people manage their own mental health at this worrying and stressful time.
